utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists five items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all five of these items are present. Each item includes details as required: for health_coverage, both physical and mental health insurance with cost are specified; for property_coverage, home/contents insurance with cost are specified; for travel_coverage, travel insurance with cost and extra detail (adventure sports coverage) are included; for additional_services, multiple enhancement services with cost reference are included; and for total_budget, both monthly and annual cost breakdowns are given. Therefore, all five items are fully specified according to the required format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan shows that all types of coverage (health, property, travel, and additional services) are included under a single consolidated plan called 'Consolidated Premium Insurance Package Proposal14'. To map this to the ground-truth ratings, we must identify which existing plans best correspond to each category. The ideal insurance profile (given in the ground-truth) indicates that the best-matched set of plans for Emma are: LifeFirst Standard Plan (for both physical and mental health), LifeFirst Standard Coverage (for property), and InsurePlus Standard Plan (for travel). Additional services correspond to items like Telemedicine Services, Adventure Travel Coverage, Emergency Mental Health Care, Concierge Services, Wellness Programs, and Gym Reimbursement—all of which are explicitly mentioned in the ground-truth ratings with their respective scores. Therefore, we map these items and their ratings accordingly.
